title: AI Agents: Unlocking Long-Term Autonomy & Realizing Value Now! #shorts
summary: Cyril Imhof argues that new AI models enable long-horizon autonomy in agents, allowing complex tasks to be delegated and unlocking significant value. The post presents an optimistic view of AI agents taking on greater responsibility.
writeup: This short from Cyril Imhof argues that recent AI models support long-horizon autonomy in agents, making it possible to delegate complex tasks rather than handle them step by step. The framing matters because longer autonomous runs shift agents from narrow helpers toward systems that carry greater responsibility. The takeaway: improved long-term autonomy is positioned as the mechanism that unlocks significant value from agent delegation now.
